feature: Home page should support guest state controls, guest navigation, and login entry from the Person button

source.notes: Imported from Azure DevOps work item 6445

observedSteps: USER STORY

As a guest user, I want the Home page to display the guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
APPLICATION CONTEXT
This application is a single page application (SPA). The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home
The Home page is the primary landing page for both unauthenticated users and authenticated users. This workflow covers only the guest-state behavior of the Home page before login.
The Home page in this workflow should be treated as the guest state of the existing Home page, not as a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
GUEST HOME STATE
When the application is launched and the user has not signed in, the Home page should load in guest state.
In guest state, the Home page displays the approved guest and shared controls, including:
Person/Profile button Back button Notification button Home button Guest-accessible feature buttons Known guest-accessible feature buttons include:
Customer signup button Clean survey button Site survey button GUEST-STATE EXPECTATIONS
In guest state:

The Person/Profile button should be visible and enabled. Clicking the Person/Profile button should open the Login page or Login view. Guest-accessible feature buttons should be visible and enabled. Guest-accessible feature buttons should navigate to their supported destinations. Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons should not be visible or should not be actionable. The logged-in user name button should not be visible in guest state. NAVIGATION EXPECTATIONS
From guest Home state:

Clicking the Person/Profile button should open the Login page. Clicking Customer signup should open the customer-search page. Clicking Clean survey should open the vehicle-survey page. Clicking Site survey should open the survey page. SHARED HOME CONTROLS
The following controls are part of the Home page shell and should be treated as Home page elements:
Back button Notification button Home button These shared controls should be modeled in the Home page resource/POM and reused across future Home page states.
VALIDATION EXPECTATIONS
This workflow should validate:

Successful initial load of the Home page in guest state Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls Visibility and interactivity of shared Home controls Person/Profile button navigation to the Login page Navigation behavior of approved guest-accessible feature buttons Absence of authenticated-only controls while the user is still unauthenticated Absence of the logged-in user name button in guest state POM / RESOURCE OWNERSHIP GUIDANCE
For automation generation and reuse:

All guest-state Home controls should belong to the Home page POM/resource. Shared Home shell controls should belong to the Home page POM/resource. The Person/Profile button belongs to the Home page POM/resource. The Login page should not own the Person/Profile button because Login is opened from Home. This workflow should establish the base Home page resource for reuse in later workflows. Later workflows should reuse this Home page resource to navigate into Login and to validate future authenticated Home state behavior. BUSINESS VALUE

This ensures the Home page guest state is modeled correctly as the initial SPA landing experience, provides reliable navigation behavior for guest users, and establishes the foundational Home page resource needed for downstream Login and authenticated Home workflows.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
Given the user launches the application and navigates to http://localhost/washtabui/home , when the page loads, then the Home page should load successfully in guest state.

Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, and approved guest-accessible feature buttons should be visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Person/Profile button, then the Login page should open.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks Customer signup, then the customer-search page should open.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks Clean survey, then the vehicle-survey page should open.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks Site survey, then the survey page should open.

Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons should not be visible or should not be actionable.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the logged-in user name button should not be visible.
